events.title: Piano Trio Gems | Needham Concert Society
events.description:
Event date: May 17, 2026 
Event Time: 03:00 PM - 11:59 PM
Location: 
800 Highland Avenue
Needham, MA 02494
Description:
Diamonds in the Rough - Violinist Irina Muresanu and pianist Asiya Korepanova join cellist Ronald Lowry in a program that brims with passion and contrast, characterized by shimmering color (Turina), fiery intensity (Babajanian), and lyrical virtuosity (Mendelssohn).

events.title: Exclusive showing:  'The Day Iceland Stood Still'
events.description:
Event date: May 21, 2026 
Event Time: 05:30 PM - 08:00 PM
Location: 
300 Hillside Avenue
Needham, MA 02494
Description:
Progressive Needham and the Center at the Heights are pleased to present an exclusive screening of the award-winning documentary, The Day Iceland Stood Still.  The film is an iconic depiction of a successful social strike.  The day in 1975 that 90% of women in Iceland ‘took the day off’ rocked the nation and began a movement that has propelled the country from the bottom to the top of world gender equality indexes.  The documentary uses personal stories and animation to show how this massive, humorous, and urgent act of solidarity launched Iceland into a global leader of gender equality.  Stay for a conversation with Fredie Kay from the Women’s History Center and Deb Paul from Neighborhood United Indivisible following the film.

events.title: Needham Garden Club Annual Plant Sale
events.description: On Saturday, 5/16/ from 9:00 – 1:00, the Needham Garden Club will host its annual Plant Sale outside of the Needham Congregational Church (1154 Great Plain Avenue, Needham). The sale will feature native plants which are hearty for our region and many plants from members’ gardens, including sun and shade perennials, grasses, herbs and ferns. Parking for the event is in the rear of the building across from the Post Office. Proceeds from the sale help sponsor educational programs in horticulture and floral design at the Needham Library; Garden Therapy sessions at local assisted living and nursing homes; a college scholarship for a Needham Senior planning to study biology, horticulture, conservation or environmental science; as well as plantings and maintenance of community garden spaces around town. With the proceeds from last year’s sale, we were also able to support the Needham High School Environmental Club and the Needham Community Farm.

events.title: How To Build An Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) in Needham
events.description: How to Build an Accessory Dwelling Unit (ADU) in Needham Join us to get your questions answered! This event—co-sponsored by the Needham Housing Coalition, Charles River Regional Chamber, Progressive Needham, League of Women Voters, and Green Needham—will explore why and how to add an ADU to your property. Date & Time: Wednesday, May 20th, 7 p.m. Location: Needham Public Library   How can you benefit from an ADU? How do you build and finance an ADU? What are the new ADU bylaw requirements?   ADUs are a valuable small housing opportunity for young people, the local workforce, seniors who are downsizing, and property owners seeking supplemental income. Recent state initiatives increase the opportunity for single family homeowners to build ADUs.   Learn from our panel of experts: Jim Sullivan, Needham Building Department Amy Wortzman, Eastern Bank Rick Leland, Remodeling remedies, LLC Oscar Mertz, Needham Planning Board Needham resident who has built an ADU Admission is free and advance registration is not required. For more information please contact needhamhousingcoalition@gmail.com.

events.title: Exclusive Showing: The Day Iceland Stood Still
events.description: Progressive Needham and the Center at the Heights are pleased to present an exclusive screening of the award-winning documentary, The Day Iceland Stood Still. The film is an iconic depiction of a successful social strike. The day in 1975 that 90% of women in Iceland ‘took the day off’ rocked the nation and began a movement that has propelled the country from the bottom to the top of world gender equality indexes. The documentary uses personal stories and animation to show how this massive, humorous, and urgent act of solidarity launched Iceland into a global leader of gender equality. Stay for a conversation with Fredie Kay from the Women’s History Center and Deb Paul from Neighborhood United Indivisible following the film.

events.title: King’s Own Come to Needham!
events.description: The British Are Coming! On Saturday, May 30th Needham will once again host The King’s Own, a group of reenactors who portray the British Redcoats who sought to prevent the American Revolution. Adults, teens, and children are invited to attend the fun, educational event that helps commemorate the 250th Anniversary of the founding of our country. See authentic uniforms, weapons, and drill, ask questions, and learn about the challenging days that led ordinary people of Needham into direct confrontation with their King and his army. Learn how British soldiers lived, fought, and made their way though seven years of warfare from Canada to the Caribbean. This event is sponsored by the Needham Cultural Council and is free to all.

events.title: High School Summer Program at Babson – Apply Now
events.description: Babson College is now accepting applications for the Arthur M. Blank School Summer Program, a pre-college experience for rising high school juniors and seniors (ages 16+). Students can choose from 50+ hands-on courses in areas like business, finance, AI, entrepreneurship, and more—offered on campus, online, or commuter. The program runs July 6–31, 2026. Space is limited, and courses fill on a rolling, first-come, first-served basis. Learn more, apply, or register for an upcoming info session: Link: https://www.babson.edu/summer-at-babson/high-school-learners/the-arthur-m-blank-school-summer-program/?utm_source=xchannel&utm_medium=link&utm_campaign=CM_0_custom_260215_product_0___AWA_&utm_content=_click

events.title: Call for Walkers: Register for the 38th Annual Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber presented by Hyundai
events.description: Registration is open for the 2026 Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber presented by Hyundai. Scheduled for Sunday, Oct. 4, funds raised from the Walk support all forms of adult and pediatric care and cancer research at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute. The 2026 event will aim to raise $10 million in support of Dana-Farber’s mission to defy cancer by leading the way in groundbreaking research and lifesaving patient care. The Details: The Jimmy Fund Walk is the only organized walk permitted to use the famed Boston Marathon® course, and participants have the flexibility to choose from four distance options: 5K walk: Starting at Dana-Farber’s Longwood Medical Campus 10K walk: Starting in Newton Half Marathon walk: Starting in Wellesley Marathon walk: Starting in Hopkinton Along the route, participants will be motivated by Hero posters, photos and quotes from Dana-Farber patients, who are considered the heart and inspiration for the event. Walkers will also enjoy refreshments and cheer squads throughout the course. The event will conclude at the Boston Common Finish Line, by the corner of Charles and Beacon Streets. Finish line activities will include a celebration with food, entertainment, and more. Walkers who prefer to participate virtually can complete their chosen distance from any location. To register for the Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber or to support a walker, visit JimmyFund.org/Walk or call (866) 531-9255. Registrants can enter the promo code NEWS for $5 off the registration fee. You can also sign up to volunteer on Walk Day.

location.venue: Boston Common by the corner of Charles and Beacon Street

events.title: Needham High School Artisan Craft Fair and Bake Sale
events.description: The 43nd Annual Needham High School Craft Fair will be taking place on Saturday, October 24, 2026, from 9 am to 3 pm at Needham High School. Come shop from over 100 Local Vendors featuring affordable, high quality unique items, and from our incredible Bake Sale with homemade favorites prepared by our wonderful community, plus donations from local restaurants & bakeries. All proceeds benefit Needham High School PTC programs that support NHS students throughout the year. For more information, please email needhamcraftfair@gmail.com
